Title: A central circadian oscillator confers defense heterosis in hybrids without growth vigor costs

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB-related transcription factor with N-terminal MYB DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0 × Sei-0 (FCS); Col-0 × Aa-0 (FCA); additional F1 hybrids
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Salicylic acid promotes antibacterial defense/systemic acquired resistance; chlorophyll supports photosynthesis and growth; starch serves as carbon/energy reserve for growth
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: salicylic acid biosynthesis; chlorophyll synthesis/photosynthesis-related metabolism; starch degradation/metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional regulation and indirect epigenetic control; activates salicylic-acid-associated defense output before dawn of first infection day and represses growth-related genes during daytime via promoter binding; rhythmic histone modification changes modulate CCA1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of PAD4, EDS1, CBP60g, SARD1, and ICS1 in hybrids at defense phase; directly represses PORB, GWD3, and DPE1 by promoter binding during daytime
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAD4; EDS1; CBP60g; SARD1; ICS1; PORB; GWD3; DPE1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential regulator that confers defense heterosis through elevated salicylic acid accumulation and simultaneously enables growth heterosis by relieving repression of chlorophyll/starch metabolism at specific times of day
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes salicylic acid accumulation and defense heterosis in F1 hybrids; rhythmically enhances chlorophyll accumulation and starch turnover to avoid growth penalties and even increase growth heterosis under pathogen infection
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCA1 showed above-high-parent expression at 21 hpi specifically in the defense-heterotic FCS hybrid after Pst DC3000 infection, correlating with elevated salicylic acid accumulation. 2. Genetic perturbation of CCA1 abolished heterosis for bacterial defense and largely eliminated growth heterosis under pathogen infection. 3. CCA1 was required for above-high-parent expression and H3Ac enrichment of key salicylic acid biosynthetic regulators/genes (PAD4, EDS1, CBP60g, SARD1, ICS1) in hybrids. 4. At 6 hpi, below-low-parent CCA1 expression in hybrids reduced its promoter occupancy on PORB, GWD3, and DPE1, allowing increased chlorophyll synthesis and starch metabolism. 5. Differential CCA1 expression at 6 hpi and 21 hpi was associated with altered H3K4me3 and H3Ac rhythms on the CCA1 locus itself.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in Sei-0 and hybrid analysis with cca1-1; pathogen inoculation with Pst DC3000 and bacterial titer measurements; qRT-PCR; salicylic acid quantification by HPLC-MS/MS; ChIP-qPCR for H3Ac, H3K4me3, and CCA1 binding; chlorophyll and starch measurements; western blot
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rhythmic circadian expression with peak around dawn; pathogen-inducible differential regulation in hybrids relative to parents
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In FCS, below-low-parent expression at 6 hpi and above-high-parent expression at 21 hpi after Pst DC3000 infection; returns to mid-parent at 24 and 45 hpi; below-low-parent pattern recurs during middle of later infection days. In FCA, below-low-parent pattern at 6 hpi but no above-high-parent induction at 21 hpi
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions within the circadian clock network; rhythmic regulation depends on evening complex components ELF3 and ELF4
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Mutual repression with TOC1 in the core clock; rhythmic regulation within the circadian feedback loop
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pathogen infection by Pst DC3000; circadian time/photocycle; histone modifications (H3Ac and H3K4me3)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 infection; dawn vs dusk inoculation; 12 h light/12 h dark photocycles
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: CCA1 expression in hybrids correlates with rhythmic histone modifications on its locus: lower H3K4me3/H3Ac at 6 hpi and higher H3K4me3/H3Ac at 21 hpi versus parents. CCA1 is also required for increased H3Ac at promoters of PAD4, EDS1, CBP60g, and SARD1 in infected hybrids.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive or mistimed activation of CCA1 would be expected to impose growth costs; in this study, precise temporal regulation avoids immunity-growth trade-offs. CCA1 mutation abolishes defense heterosis and strongly reduces growth heterosis under infection.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value regulator for engineering hybrids/crops with stronger durable PTI-like disease resistance without yield penalties; potential strategies include pathogen-inducible or time-specific promoters to elevate CCA1 at appropriate times, or selecting parental CCA1 alleles that generate favorable heterotic expression patterns in hybrids
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Promoter motif analysis and DEG set from previous transcriptomic dataset of infected FCS hybrids (Supplementary Data 1 in cited prior study); no accession reported in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the sole core circadian loop factor decisive for defense heterosis in this study; LHY and TOC1 were tested but did not affect defense/growth heterosis, whereas ELF3 and ELF4 influenced CCA1 rhythmic differential expression. CCA1 regulates nutrient-relevant metabolites indirectly; it is not a biosynthetic enzyme.
